text:
CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
CT CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 49-year-old female with history of FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC). Status post primary debulking surgery (bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, total abdominal hysterectomy, radical omentectomy, pelvic and para-aortic lymph node dissection) with R0 resection, followed by adjuvant Paraplatin/PTX x 6 cycles with CA-125 normalization and complete radiographic response. Currently on nira maintenance therapy (100 mg daily, dose-reduced). Annual surveillance imaging at 24-month mark from initial diagnosis. CA-125 most recently 12 U/mL. Rule out recurrent disease.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest was performed with intravenous contrast (Omnipaque 350, 100 mL IV). Multidetector CT of the abdomen and pelvis was performed with intravenous contrast (same bolus, portal venous phase acquisition). Oral contrast was not administered. Axial images were reconstructed at 3 mm intervals throughout. Multiplanar reformats (coronal and sagittal) were reviewed. Images were reviewed on soft tissue, lung, and bone window settings.

COMPARISON: Prior CT abdomen/pelvis and CT chest from approximately 6 months ago (surveillance imaging at 14 months from diagnosis, 6 months into nira maintenance). Also compared to the post-chemotherapy end-of-treatment restaging CT performed approximately 10 months prior.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are well-aerated bilaterally. The central airways are patent. No pulmonary nodules are identified. No focal areas of airspace consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or atelectasis. No parenchymal masses. The lung parenchyma appears within normal limits. Scattered mild subsegmental linear opacities at the lung bases bilaterally, likely representing minimal atelectasis or scarring, unchanged and without clinical significance.

Pleural Spaces: No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pleural thickening. No pleural nodularity. The pleural surfaces are smooth and unremarkable on both sides.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is midline. The thymus is not enlarged. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy. The superior mediastinal, precarinal, subcarinal, and bilateral hilar lymph nodes are not enlarged; the largest visualized node measures approximately 6 mm in the subcarinal region, well within normal limits and unchanged from prior. No hilar masses.

Heart and Pericardium: The cardiac silhouette is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. The great vessels are unremarkable.

Chest Wall and Thoracic Skeleton: The chest wall soft tissues are unremarkable. No suspicious osseous lesions in the visualized ribs, sternum, or thoracic vertebral bodies. The visualized osseous structures are within normal limits.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and morphology. The hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal areas of hypo- or hyperattenuation suspicious for metastatic deposit. No hepatic nodules or masses identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. The gallbladder is surgically absent (cholecystectomy changes noted, consistent with prior surgical history). Portal and hepatic venous structures enhance appropriately.

Spleen: The spleen is normal in size (craniocaudal span approximately 10.1 cm) and demonstrates homogeneous parenchymal enhancement. No focal splenic lesions. No perisplenic fluid collection or nodularity along the splenic capsule.

Pancreas: The pancreas is visualized to its entirety and appears normal. No focal pancreatic masses or ductal dilatation. Peripancreatic fat planes are preserved.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands are of normal morphology and size. No adrenal nodules or masses. No adrenal enlargement.

Kidneys and Ureters: The kidneys are normal in size, contour, and cortical thickness bilaterally. Normal cortical enhancement and excretion. No solid renal masses. No hydronephrosis. No perinephric stranding. No ureteral dilatation to suggest obstruction.

Omentum and Mesentery: This is a critically assessed region given the patient's history of omental carcinomatosis. The omentum has been surgically resected (radical omentectomy). The remaining mesenteric and retroperitoneal fat demonstrates expected post-surgical appearance. No new soft tissue nodularity, infiltration, or mass lesion is identified within the mesentery or residual omental fat. The fat planes are clean throughout. There is no stellate retraction or cake-like thickening to suggest peritoneal seeding.

Peritoneal Surfaces: The peritoneal surfaces throughout the abdomen are carefully scrutinized. No peritoneal implants identified. No nodular thickening of the parietal or visceral peritoneum. No soft tissue deposits along the right or left paracolic gutters. No evidence of tumor studding along the serosal surfaces of the small bowel or colon. The right hemidiaphragm peritoneum, which was stripped surgically, shows expected post-operative fibrous change without suspicious nodularity or new soft tissue proliferation. There is no recurrent peritoneal carcinomatosis.

Ascites: No ascites. The peritoneal cavity is entirely dry. This contrasts favorably with the large-volume ascites (approximately 4-4.5 liters) present at initial diagnosis.

Retroperitoneal Lymph Nodes: The retroperitoneal lymph node chains are carefully assessed. The para-aortic lymph nodes at the infrarenal level, the site of prior pathologically confirmed nodal involvement (2/12 nodes positive at surgery), are not enlarged. Individual lymph nodes in this region are discrete and measure no more than 7 mm in short axis, within normal limits and not meaningfully changed from prior surveillance imaging. No new or enlarged retroperitoneal lymph nodes identified. The paraaortic, paracaval, and interaortocaval regions are clear.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The stomach and visualized loops of small bowel are normal in caliber without wall thickening, obstruction, or adjacent inflammatory change. The colon appears unremarkable. The appendix is surgically absent (appendectomy). No free air. The rectum and sigmoid colon, which were inspected at surgery for mesenteric implants, appear unremarkable without wall thickening or peri-colonic soft tissue changes.

Abdominal Vasculature: The aorta and inferior vena cava are normal in caliber. No vascular abnormalities identified.

---

PELVIS:

Gynecologic Structures: The uterus and bilateral adnexa have been surgically removed (total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy confirmed on prior imaging and clinical history). No residual gynecologic soft tissue masses or recurrence in the operative bed. The vaginal cuff is intact with expected post-surgical appearance, without nodular or mass-like soft tissue thickening to suggest cuff recurrence. The vaginal apex is well-defined.

Bladder: The urinary bladder is adequately distended with homogeneous enhancement of the wall. No bladder wall thickening, intraluminal mass, or adjacent soft tissue abnormality.

Pelvic Peritoneum and Cul-de-Sac: The cul-de-sac (pouch of Douglas) is clear. No soft tissue deposit or nodularity along the pelvic peritoneum. No fluid in the dependent pelvis. The pelvic fat planes, including the pre-sacral and paravesical spaces, are clean without infiltration.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: The bilateral external iliac, internal iliac, and obturator lymph node chains are assessed. The right external iliac chain, the site of pathologically confirmed lymph node involvement (4 positive nodes at surgery, largest 2.1 cm), no longer shows lymph nodes of pathologic size. No discrete enlarged pelvic lymph nodes are identified. Individual nodes are small, flat, and within normal limits. No new pelvic lymphadenopathy.

Bony Pelvis and Visualized Spine: The osseous structures of the pelvis, including the sacrum, iliac wings, and visualized lumbar vertebral bodies, are intact without lytic or blastic lesions suspicious for osseous metastatic disease. Bone density appears within normal limits for age. Post-surgical changes are noted along the anterior abdominal wall at the midline laparotomy site, with expected subcutaneous fibrotic changes and no hernia identified.

Port-a-Cath: A right-sided chest wall subcutaneous port-a-cath device is present. The catheter tip projects in the expected location of the superior vena cava/right atrial junction. No associated soft tissue abnormality or complication identified.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of recurrent high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma at 24-month surveillance. The peritoneal surfaces, pelvic and para-aortic lymph node stations, hepatic parenchyma, and pleural spaces remain entirely clear, consistent with durable complete response.

2. No ascites, no peritoneal implants, no omental nodularity, no new or enlarged lymph nodes in the retroperitoneum or pelvis. All previously involved sites (right paracolic gutter, left paracolic gutter, right hemidiaphragm, sigmoid mesentery, bilateral pelvic nodes, infrarenal para-aortic nodes) appear within normal limits without evidence of recurrent soft tissue deposits.

3. The vaginal cuff is unremarkable without soft tissue thickening or recurrence.

4. No pulmonary nodules, no pleural effusion, no mediastinal lymphadenopathy on CT chest.

5. Stable post-surgical changes from prior debulking procedure. Right chest wall port-a-cath in satisfactory position.

6. Mild lower lobe linear atelectasis bilaterally, not clinically significant.

Overall, this annual surveillance CT demonstrates maintained complete radiographic response with no imaging findings to suggest disease recurrence at 24 months from initial diagnosis. These findings are concordant with normalized serum CA-125.
